hallucinations.description: Multiple references to 2025 papers (e.g., Subramanyam et al. (2025), Thrush et al. (2025), Myntti et al. (2025), Diao et al. (2025), Shum et al. (2025), Messmer et al. (2025)) with fabricated arXiv IDs and titles that do not correspond to real publications. These appear invented to support the narrative on data quality scaling laws and perplexity filtering.
hallucinations.evidence: References section lists 'Subramanyam, A., Chen, Y., Grossman, R. L. (2025). Scaling Laws Revisited... arXiv:2510.03313', 'Thrush, T., Potts, C., Hashimoto, T. (2025). Improving Pretraining Data Using Perplexity Correlations. ICLR 2025. arXiv:2409.05816', and similar entries for Myntti, Diao, Shum, Messmer.

overall_assessment: Hallucinations found: several nonexistent/fake 2025 citations. Experimental results, methodology, and numbers align with provided code/execution logs and appear genuine. Severity is moderate (citations only).
